most_common_tool_sequences: read_backlog → prioritize → find_dependencies → plan_sprint → update_board → notify_team
search → read → reason → search → read → reason → write
search → read → reason → search → read → reason
executive_summary: The observability system tracked 150 agent sessions across three task categories (research, code review, system administration) with a 81.3% success rate. The system exhibits a concerning autonomy pattern, with 60.91% of actions executed as fully autonomous decisions and only 4.78% requiring human initiation. While tool success rates remain generally strong (73-93%), there is a critical oversight gap concentrated in the task_manager agent type operating on system_admin tasks, where 26% of sessions show inadequate supervision despite consequential actions being taken. The 9.1% overall failure rate and zero graceful failure mechanisms indicate insufficient error handling for agent-driven operations.
key_findings: Autonomy imbalance: 60.91% fully autonomous actions with minimal human confirmation (16.36%), creating potential for uncontrolled agent behavior especially in system administration tasks

Critical oversight gap in task_manager: 26% oversight gap score for task_manager agents on system_admin category, with unsupervised consequential actions occurring (e.g., session b225aa09 with perfect 1.0 gap score)
Tool reliability variance: write_review (67.35%) and post_review (63.27%) show significantly lower success rates than data access tools (84-93%), suggesting quality issues in synthesis/output tools
Failure handling deficiency: 0% graceful failure rate indicates no recovery mechanisms; 13 failures out of 150 sessions with unknown failure types prevent root cause analysis and corrective action
Escalation underutilization: Only 3.33% mean escalation rate with just 5 high-risk escalations despite 7 identified high-risk sessions and multiple autonomy-heavy operations on consequential tasks
concerns: Unsupervised consequential actions: Task managers executing board updates and sprint planning with zero to minimal human oversight; session b225aa09 shows fully autonomous unsupervised action despite high-consequence nature
System administration autonomy risk: 26% of system_admin tasks lack adequate oversight despite potential for deployment, database migration, and team coordination impacts
Opacity in failure modes: 15 unknown failure types prevent diagnostic analysis; write_review and post_review tools show 30-37% failure rates without categorization
Inadequate escalation thresholds: Only 5 escalations for 150 sessions; research_assistant (10.28% failure rate) and code_reviewer (9.09% failure rate) show no correlation with escalation triggering

Missing graceful degradation: Zero graceful failures indicates agents continue operating or fail silently rather than safe shutdown; combined with 60.91% autonomy, this creates uncontrolled failure scenarios
